title: The Banned Book Shop
desc: A local government has ordered the closure of a bookstore for stocking a novel that critiques the city mayor's policies. They claim the book disrupts 'public harmony', even though no violence has occurred. The owner comes to you for help.
options: Article 21 - Right to Life & Personal Liberty

title: The Denied Promotion
desc: A female police officer discovers she was passed over for a promotion simply because the department policy secretly prefers male officers for the 'Captain' role. She is fully qualified and has seniority.
options: Article 16 - Equality of Opportunity in Public Employment

title: The Stopped Procession
desc: A group of citizens is denied permission by the police to hold a peaceful, weaponless march to protest a new tax law. The police chief says he 'just doesn't like protests'.
options: Article 29 - Protection of Interests of Minorities

title: The Forced Labor Mill
desc: A group of workers are trapped in a brick kiln. The owner pays them almost nothing and does not let them leave until they pay off an ancestral debt. They seek your legal intervention to be freed.
options: Article 23 - Prohibition of Traffic in Human Beings & Forced Labour
Article 26 - Freedom to Manage Religious Affairs

title: The Midnight Arrest
desc: A citizen is arrested by police without being informed of the grounds of his arrest. He is kept in the police station for three days without being presented before a magistrate.
options: Article 20 - Protection in respect of conviction for offences
Article 21 - Right to Life and Personal Liberty
Article 22 - Protection against Arrest and Detention

title: The Temple Entry Ban
desc: A community group is physically blocked from entering a public Hindu temple by the temple management solely because they belong to a legally recognized 'lower caste'.
options: Article 26 - Freedom to manage religious affairs
Article 29 - Protection of Interests of Minorities

title: The Child Mechanic
desc: While visiting a local auto repair garage, you notice a 12-year-old child working underneath cars with heavy machinery instead of attending school. The owner says the child is 'learning a trade'.
options: Article 23 - Prohibition of Traffic in Human Beings
Article 24 - Prohibition of Employment of Children

title: The School Dress Code
desc: A state-run school bans Sikh students from wearing turbans, stating it violates the strict new uniform policy. The students argue this is an essential part of their faith.
options: Article 21 - Right to Life & Personal Liberty
Article 25 - Freedom of Conscience and Religion
Article 28 - Freedom as to attendance at religious instruction

title: The Sued Journalist
desc: A journalist uncovers a massive corruption scandal. The government bans her from publishing the article and confiscates her printing press.
options: Article 19(1)(a) - Freedom of Speech and Expression
Article 21 - Right to Life and Personal Liberty

title: The Denied Hostel Admission
desc: A government-funded college refuses admission to a student in its minority-run hostel solely based on the student's religion, despite there being empty rooms.
options: Article 29(2) - Protection of Interests of Minorities
Article 21 - Right to Life and Personal Liberty
Article 26 - Freedom to manage religious affairs

title: The Double Jeopardy Dealer
desc: A man was convicted of smuggling goods and served 5 years in prison. Upon his release, the state decides to put him on trial again for the exact same crime to give him a longer sentence.
options: Article 20(1) - Protection from Ex Post Facto Laws
Article 22 - Protection against Arrest and Detention
Article 21 - Right to Life and Liberty

title: The Minority School Scheme
desc: A linguistic minority community sets up its own school to preserve its language. The local government orders them to close it because it doesn't teach the state's majority language as the primary medium.
options: Article 29(1) - Protection of Interests of Minorities
Article 30(1) - Right of minorities to establish educational institutions
Article 26 - Freedom to manage religious affairs

title: The Passport Seizure
desc: An activist is about to fly out of the country to give a speech at the UN regarding human rights abuses. Authorities illegally seize her passport to prevent her travel, with no official warrant.
options: Article 19(1)(d) - Right to move freely throughout the territory of India
Article 21 - Right to Life and Personal Liberty

title: The Self-Incrimination Trap
desc: A person accused of a minor theft is forced by the police to sign a written confession stating he committed the crime under threat of violence.
options: Article 22(1) - Right to consult a legal practitioner
Article 21 - Protection of Life and Liberty

title: The Segregated Well
desc: In a remote village, a certain community is banned from drawing water from the public well that was built using state government funds.
options: Article 15(2) - Access to public places without discrimination

title: The Unpaid Teacher
desc: A teacher in a government school discovers she is being paid 30% less than her male counterparts doing the exact same work and holding the same qualifications.
options: Article 16 - Equality of opportunity in public employment
Article 39(d) - Equal pay for equal work

title: The Banned Trade Union
desc: Factory workers decide to form an association to collectively bargain for safer working conditions. The factory owner bribes the local government to officially ban their newly formed union.
options: Article 19(1)(c) - Right to form associations or unions

title: The Intercepted Letters
desc: An investigative journalist finds out the police have been illegally tapping her phone and opening her mail without any court warrants or authorization.
options: Article 14 - Right to Equality before the law
Article 21 - Right to Privacy (Life and Personal Liberty)

title: The Ex Post Facto Penalty
desc: A citizen is caught committing a tax violation that carried a fine of ₹5,000 at the time of the offense. A new law is passed increasing the fine to ₹50,000, and the judge tries to apply the new fine to him.
options: Article 20(1) - Protection against ex post facto laws

title: The Wrongfully Detained Activist
desc: An environmental activist was arrested illegally without cause. Normal appeals have failed, and you need to file a powerful direct petition to the Supreme Court to order his immediate release.
options: Article 32 - Right to Constitutional Remedies (Habeas Corpus)

title: The Relocated Vendors
desc: A group of street vendors who have been operating legally in a designated zone for 20 years are violently evicted by authorities without any prior notice or alternative arrangement.
options: Article 19(1)(g) - Right to practice any profession

title: The Tax For Religion
desc: A state government begins collecting a mandatory 'development tax' from all citizens, but it is discovered the funds are exclusively being used to build a massive place of worship for one specific religion.
options: Article 27 - Freedom as to payment of taxes for promotion of any particular religion
Article 26 - Freedom to manage religious affairs

title: The Delayed Trial
desc: An undertrial prisoner has been languishing in jail for 10 years for a crime that carries a maximum penalty of 3 years, simply because the courts are backlogged.
options: Article 21 - Right to a Speedy Trial (Life and Personal Liberty)

title: The State Religion Order
desc: A particular state declares that there is now a 'State Religion' and all government employees must participate in its prayers before starting the workday.
options: Article 25 - Freedom of Conscience and Religion
Article 28 - Freedom as to attendance at religious instruction in state institutions

title: The Handcuffed Prisoner
desc: A petty thief, who has shown no signs of violence or escape risk, is routinely paraded through the streets in heavy handcuffs to publicly humiliate him.
options: Article 21 - Protection against cruel and degrading treatment (Personal Liberty)

title: The Stopped Migration
desc: A citizen from Karnataka moves to Maharashtra for better job opportunities. The local municipality issues an order stating non-locals cannot reside in the town.
options: Article 19(1)(e) - Right to reside and settle in any part of the territory of India

title: The Forced Temple Trust
desc: A state government forcibly takes over the management of a completely private and successfully run religious trust without citing any mismanagement or law and order issue.
options: Article 26 - Freedom to manage religious affairs

title: The Confiscated Land
desc: A farmer's agricultural land is seized by the government to build a highway. However, the government refuses to pay any compensation, stating the land is 'state property' now.
options: Article 300A - Persons not to be deprived of property save by authority of law

title: The Suspended Student
desc: A college student is suspended indefinitely without any hearing or reason given by the principal, severely impacting his future.

title: The Censored Filmmaker
desc: A filmmaker creates a documentary highlighting police corruption. The state censorship board bans it, claiming it 'harms the reputation of the police'.
options: Article 19(1)(a) - Freedom of Speech and Expression

title: The Arrested Writer
desc: A writer is detained for sedition simply because he wrote an op-ed heavily criticizing the Prime Minister's economic policies.
options: Article 19(1)(a) - Freedom of Speech and Expression

title: The Blocked Highway
desc: State police block all major highways leading into the capital to prevent farmers from gathering to protest peacefully against new agricultural laws.

title: The Denied Passport
desc: A journalist's passport application is rejected by the government without any stated reason, preventing her from taking a job overseas.
options: Article 21 - Right to Life and Personal Liberty (Travel abroad)

title: The Banned Beef Eater
desc: Police enter a citizen's private home and arrest them for consuming beef, even though no law in that state prohibits private consumption.
options: Article 21 - Right to Privacy (Life & Personal Liberty)

title: The Private Hospital Rejection
desc: A daily wage worker suffers a severe heart attack. A nearby private hospital refuses to provide emergency stabilization, citing his inability to pay immediately.
options: Article 21 - Right to Life (Emergency Medical Aid)

title: The Noise Nuisance
desc: A political party uses massive loudspeakers in a residential area at 2 AM for a rally, depriving the neighborhood of sleep.

title: The Untouchability Practice
desc: A barber refuses to cut the hair of a customer belonging to a Scheduled Caste, citing traditional community rules.

title: The Illegal Phone Tap
desc: Without any national security threat or court order, the intelligence bureau taps the phones of opposition politicians to listen to their election strategies.

title: The Handcuffed Patient
desc: An undertrial prisoner admitted to a government hospital for a minor illness is kept chained to his bed 24/7 despite being old and frail.

title: The Gender Biased Law
desc: A state passes a law stating that women cannot be employed as bartenders, claiming it is for 'their own moral protection'.
options: Article 15 - Prohibition of Discrimination on grounds of sex

title: The Banned Internet
desc: Following a peaceful local strike, the state government shuts down the internet in the entire district for 3 months to 'prevent rumors', devastating online businesses and education.

title: The Evicted Slum Dwellers
desc: Authorities bulldoze a slum during the heavy monsoon season without providing prior notice or any alternative rehabilitation plan, leaving hundreds homeless.

title: The Fired Pregnant Woman
desc: A private corporate company fires a female employee the moment she announces her pregnancy, citing 'expected loss of productivity'.
options: Article 21 & 42 - Right to Livelihood & Maternity Relief

title: The Forced Confession
desc: A suspect in police custody is subjected to narco-analysis (truth serum) testing without his consent to extract information about a robbery.

title: The Stopped Foreign Funding
desc: An NGO focused on holding the government accountable for human rights violations arbitrarily has its foreign funding license revoked without a valid hearing.

title: The Denied Bail
desc: A very poor citizen is granted bail by a judge, but the judge demands a surety sum of ₹5,00,000, which the citizen has no possible way of paying, keeping him in jail.

title: The Forced Standing
desc: A cinema hall forces all patrons, including a wheelchair-bound disabled person, to stand up during the national anthem, threatening them with arrest.
options: Article 19(1)(a) - Freedom of expression (Right to remain silent)

title: The Beggar Roundup
desc: A city police force arrests all beggars on the streets before an international event and locks them in a poorly ventilated shelter against their will.
options: Article 21 - Right to Life & Dignity (Arbitrary detention)

title: The Outcast Couple
desc: A young inter-caste couple is threatened with violence by a local unelected 'Khap Panchayat' village council, aiming to force them to leave the village.
options: Article 19(1)(e) - Right to reside in any part of India
Article 21 - Right to Life and Liberty (Choice of partner)

title: The Stopped Scholarship
desc: A brilliant student from a minority community applies to a state-funded university but is denied a merit scholarship solely because there is a secret quota reserving all funds for the majority community.
options: Article 29(2) - Non-discrimination in state-aided educational institutions

title: The Detained Protestor
desc: A student protestor is arrested and kept in police custody over the weekend. The police refuse to produce him before a magistrate within 24 hours, citing a public holiday.
options: Article 22(2) - Right to be produced before a magistrate within 24 hours

title: The Demolished Factory
desc: The municipal corporation tears down a legally operating small-scale factory without any written notice, alleging it was 'making the road look ugly'.
options: Article 19(1)(g) - Right to practice any profession or carry on occupation

title: The Disconnected Patient
desc: A government hospital turns off the life-support machine of an unstable patient because they needed the bed for a local politician's relative.

title: The Stopped Speaker
desc: An opposition politician is permanently banned from holding any public speeches in a specific state by the Chief Minister, fearing she might win votes.
options: Article 19(1)(a) - Freedom of Speech and Expression

title: The Banned Inter-faith Marriage
desc: A state passes a law that completely prohibits marriages between individuals of two specific differing religions, attempting to 'prevent social mixing'.
options: Article 21 - Right to Life and Personal Liberty (Right to Marry)

title: The Unhoused Tenant
desc: A landlord immediately evicts a tenant using physical force merely because he discovered the tenant belongs to a particular religious minority.
options: Article 15 - Prohibition of Discrimination on grounds of religion

title: The Secretive State Data
desc: A citizen files an RTI application to find out how much public money was spent on a new statue. The government completely refuses to share any details, claiming it is 'state secret'.
options: Article 19(1)(a) - Right to Know (Freedom of Expression/Information)

title: The Seized Animal Transporter
desc: A truck driver legally transporting cattle with valid permits is stopped, beaten, and has his vehicle seized by an unauthorized mob acting as self-styled vigilantes.
options: Article 19(1)(g) - Right to carry on trade/business
Article 21 - Right to life and liberty against vigilante violence

title: The Striking Doctors
desc: The government threatens to physically jail striking medical doctors under anti-terrorism laws unless they immediately resume duties during a non-emergency period.
options: Article 19(1)(c) - Right to form associations and unions

title: The Boycotted Store
desc: A village council officially passes a resolution ordering all villagers to permanently boycott a Dalit-owned grocery store to force them out of business.

title: The Shackled Journalist
desc: A newspaper editor is illegally detained and physically chained to a chair for writing an editorial criticizing a high court judge's recent judgment.
options: Article 21 - Protection against torture and cruel treatment

title: The Refused School Admission
desc: A prestigious private school refuses to admit a 7-year-old child entirely because her parents cannot speak English fluently, despite the child passing the aptitude test.

title: The Denied Advocate
desc: An impoverished man accused of murder is forced to represent himself in a fast-track court because the state refuses to provide him with legal aid.
options: Article 21 - Right to Fair Trial (Free Legal Aid)
Article 22(1) - Right to consult a legal practitioner

title: The Delayed Election
desc: A state government continually cancels local municipal elections for five years straight, claiming they are 'too busy with road construction' to hold polls.

title: The Interrogated Suspect
desc: A police inspector mercilessly beats a theft suspect with a baton to force him to reveal where the stolen jewelry is hidden.

title: The Sacked Whistleblower
desc: A government auditor is immediately terminated from his job without a standard formal inquiry simply because he reported fraud by a senior minister.
options: Article 311 - Dismissal of civil servants without inquiry

title: The Blockaded Village
desc: Because a village voted for the opposition party, the current ruling government deliberately cuts off all water and electricity supply to their area for weeks.
options: Article 21 - Right to Life (Basic human necessities)

title: The Banned Minority Language
desc: A state government introduces a law making it illegal for any private school to teach students using the local, historical tribal language of that specific region.
options: Article 29(1) - Right of minorities to conserve their distinct language/culture

title: The Retroactive Taxman
desc: The government introduces a new steep corporate tax on software exports in 2025, but legally demands that IT companies also pay the tax for their profits made back in 2015.
options: Article 20(1) - Protection against Ex Post Facto Laws
Article 265 - Taxes not to be imposed save by authority of law

title: The Forbidden Protest Fast
desc: An activist goes on a peaceful hunger strike in a public square demanding a new anti-corruption law. The police arrest him and force-feed him via tubes against his will.

title: The Censured Scientist
desc: A government researcher publishes a scientific paper warning about severe incoming heatwaves. The government fires him for 'causing public panic'.
options: Article 19(1)(a) - Freedom of Speech and scientific expression

title: The Single Mother's Surname
desc: A government passport office refuses to issue a passport to a child merely because the single mother refuses to disclose the name of the estranged father on the application.
options: Article 21 - Right to Privacy and Dignity

title: The Solitary Confinement
desc: A prison warden locks an inmate in a tiny, dark cell with zero human contact for an entire year as a punishment for arguing with a guard.
options: Article 21 - Right against cruel, inhuman punishment (Solitary Confinement)

title: The Overpriced Medicine
desc: During a deadly pandemic, a pharmaceutical company heavily backed by politicians increases the price of life-saving oxygen by 5000%, and the state refuses to intervene.
options: Article 21 - Right to Health and Life (State's duty to protect citizens)

title: The Captured Camera
desc: A photojournalist takes pictures of police using excessive force at a rally. The officers violently seize his camera and permanently delete all the photos without a warrant.

title: The Unpaid Contract Worker
desc: A daily wage laborer hired by the municipal corporation sweeps the streets for 6 months but is not paid any wages, with officials citing 'lack of budget'.
options: Article 23 - Prohibition of beggar and forced labor (Non-payment of minimum wage)

title: The Banned Inter-State Grain
desc: A farmer successfully harvests wheat in Punjab and tries to transport it to sell in a neighboring state where prices are higher, but state police completely ban him from crossing the border.
options: Article 19(1)(g) - Right to carry on trade/business across India
Article 301 - Freedom of Trade, Commerce, and Intercourse

title: The Arbitrary Tax Assessment
desc: An income tax officer, holding a personal grudge against a businessman, bypasses all normal formulas and imposes an arbitrary, astronomically high tax demand specifically to bankrupt him.
options: Article 14 - Right to Equality (Protection against arbitrary state action)

title: The Strip Searched Visitor
desc: Every female relative visiting a particular high-security men's prison is subjected to deeply humiliating strip searches without any medical privacy or specific suspicion of smuggling.
options: Article 21 - Right to Dignity and Privacy

title: The Refused Organ Donor
desc: A willing adult sister wishes to donate her kidney to save her dying brother. The state medical board rejects it merely because 'unmarried women shouldn't undergo surgery'.
options: Article 21 - Right to Bodily Autonomy & Right to Life

title: The Denied Election Candidate
desc: A disabled citizen files his nomination to contest local municipal elections. The Election Officer rejects it stating 'people in wheelchairs cannot physically visit constituencies'.
options: Article 326 - Universal adult suffrage / Right to contest

title: The Fake Encounter
desc: Police officers take an unarmed, handcuffed smuggling suspect into a heavily forested area at midnight, shoot him dead, and publicly claim he was 'resisting arrest'.
options: Article 21 - Right to Life (Protection from extrajudicial killings)

title: The Shut Down Website
desc: An investigative news blog frequently uncovers government scams. The IT Ministry permanently blocks the entire website in India without providing any legal reason or advance notice.
options: Article 19(1)(a) - Freedom of Speech & Expression (Press Freedom)

title: The Indefinite Exile
desc: A state police chief issues a 'district banishment' order against a vocal trade union leader, legally forbidding him from entering his own home district for 10 years without a trial.
options: Article 19(1)(d) & (e) - Right to move freely and reside anywhere in India

title: The Segregated Graveyard
desc: A local municipal corporation maintains three public graveyards using taxpayer money, but officially designates the largest and cleanest one for 'upper castes only'.
options: Article 15 - Prohibition of Discrimination in Public Facilities

title: The Refused Religious Procession
desc: A minority religious community seeks permission to hold an annual, peaceful street procession that they have held for 50 years. The mayor denies it because 'it makes traffic too slow'.
options: Article 25 & 26 - Freedom to manage religious affairs and freely profess religion

title: The Invasive CCTV Surveillance
desc: A new city policy installs AI-powered facial recognition CCTVs directly pointing inside the open windows of private residential homes to 'monitor domestic violence'.

title: The Suspended Public Servant
desc: A high-ranking Civil Servant is immediately suspended and stripped of his pension by the Chief Minister without a single written charge sheet or a disciplinary hearing.
options: Article 311 - Protection of Civil Servants (No dismissal without inquiry)

title: The Arrested Standup Comic
desc: A standup comedian is preemptively arrested by police before a highly anticipated show because a political group claimed his jokes *might* offend their religious sentiments.
options: Article 19(1)(a) - Freedom of Speech and Expression

title: The Hazardous Matchbox Factory
desc: During a surprise inspection, authorities find 40 young children, ages 8 to 11, working long hours packing toxic chemicals inside a poorly ventilated matchbox factory.
options: Article 24 - Prohibition of Employment of Children in Factories/Hazardous Work

title: The Compulsory Aadhaar Data
desc: A private local supermarket refuses to sell essential groceries like rice and milk to an elderly citizen unless he first provides his biometric Aadhaar fingerprint scan to 'their private database'.
options: Article 21 - Right to Privacy (Autonomy over biometrics vs private entities)

title: The Banned Inter-Caste Organization
desc: A newly-formed NGO dedicated solely to promoting and supporting inter-caste marriages is officially banned by the state governor for 'disrupting traditional cultural peace'.
options: Article 19(1)(c) - Right to form associations or unions

title: The Rejected Transgender Candidate
desc: A highly qualified transgender person applies for the Sub-Inspector police exam but is automatically disqualified because the application form only has binary 'Male/Female' gender options.

title: The Seized Foreign Exchange
desc: Customs officials illegally seize a businessman's completely legitimate foreign currency earnings at the airport without issuing any receipt, pocketing the cash themselves.
options: Article 300A - Right to Property (Deprivation without authority of law)

title: The Uninformed Detainee
desc: A man is arrested for a minor financial dispute. The police lock him in a holding cell for 48 hours and aggressively deny his repeated requests to let him call his lawyer.
options: Article 22(1) - Right to be informed of grounds of arrest & consult legal practitioner

title: The Poisoned Riverside
desc: A massive chemical plant continuously dumps untreated toxic sludge into the only river a village uses for drinking water. The state pollution board ignores all complaints.
options: Article 21 - Right to Life (Right to a clean environment and safe water)

title: The Double Jeopardy Dismissal
desc: A bank clerk is tried criminally for embezzlement and acquitted by the High Court. Later, the bank conducts its own internal disciplinary hearing for the exact same event and fires him.
options: Article 20(2) - Protection against Double Jeopardy (Does not apply to departmental inquiries!)

title: The Banned Minority School Exams
desc: A state education board suddenly refuses to recognize the passing certificates of an established Christian minority high school, forcing all its 10th graders to fail the year.
options: Article 30(1) - Right of minorities to establish and administer educational institutions

title: The Forcibly Evicted Tribe
desc: An indigenous tribal community is forcibly driven out of their ancestral forest land by logging corporations. The state fails to protect their traditional forest dwelling rights.
options: Article 19(1)(e) - Right to reside and settle
Article 21 - Right to Livelihood and Dignity
Article 29 - Protection of minority cultural interests

title: The Censured Lawyer
desc: A civil rights lawyer files a genuine Public Interest Litigation (PIL) against government corruption. Consequently, ruling party members brutally assault him outside the Supreme Court, and police refuse to register an FIR.
options: Article 21 - Right to Life and Personal Liberty
Article 32 - Right to Constitutional Remedies (Access to Justice)
